title: phyllanthus compressus
intro: Phyllanthus compressus, a type of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, is commonly known as the dwarf green garlic or small-leaved green garlic. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating liver and kidney disorders, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. The health benefits of Phyllanthus compressus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Phyllanthus compressus can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, with the tea being the most common form of preparation, made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water.
